translated: Alright, just like before, I've picked out some scenes that I personally want to talk about, so let's take a look at them together.
Here they are.
It's so good.
It's just really good.
That's what I think.
Since I've already chosen my own career path, seeing the moment someone else makes their choice,
even if it's just through an event story, it makes me feel like...
well, there are definitely moments where you wonder if you're doing the right thing.
I'm sure everyone has those moments.
But if you can look back in 10 or 20 years
and think, "I'm glad I made that choice back then,"
then I think that's a great thing. And I believe effort is what makes that possible.
When it comes to things that require effort, I don't think there's such a thing as a "wrong" choice.
If you put in the effort, you might still have moments a year or two later
where you think, "Maybe I shouldn't have done this."
But looking back after 10 or 20 years, I'm sure there are times where it all connects in the end.
You'll think, "If I hadn't done that then, I wouldn't be able to do this now."
Even in different jobs, I'm sure people feel that. For those currently working hard,
or those who want to find something to work hard at, I hope this event story can be a catalyst for you to make that decision.
With that said, I'd like to talk about the illustrations now.
Let me read a message.
This one is from Meshi-san. Thank you very much.
Thank you.
"Hello, Sato-san."
"I'm always having fun playing ProSeka."
"The 4-star illustrations this time, especially Mizuki's, were so good that I went all out on the gacha"
"to make sure I got them."
"Just like in Secret Distance or the light music club song, Michi wa... Izuko?"
"Izuko."
"...it's an illustration of Mizuki in a space with torsos." Torsos appear quite often in Mizuki's illustrations.
Not mannequins, but the ones for clothes, like this.
Like, right here. Right here.
Right here.
This is called a torso.
"Reaching for an Unraveling Tomorrow" also had torsos.
It's an illustration where Mizuki is in a space filled with them.
"The torso space usually represents Mizuki's dark feelings,"
"but this time, they're looking forward after feeling the warmth of family and friends."
"It's become a space that shows Mizuki's bright and cheerful feelings, and I'm so happy to see"
"this change in Mizuki's heart."
"I can't thank the staff enough for creating such a wonderful scenario, and the voice actors"
"for giving the characters such deep and wonderful expressions."
"Thank you for always making such great events."
"Please take care of yourselves and keep up the good work."
"I'll be rooting for you as a fellow Tofu," they wrote.
Meshi-san, thank you so much.
The colors in the illustrations are quite rare for Niigo, or how should I put it...
We've had pale colors before, but seeing Niigo bathed in sunlight...
is rare, probably because they don't go outside much.
I thought seeing Niigo during the day was unusual, and this specific art style is quite new.
Everyone's cards were great, not just Mizuki's, both before and after training.
The artists are truly amazing.
I'm really grateful. We also have some comments from the illustration team about the outfits this time,
so I'd like to share those as well.
The comment says, "Pure brightness is quite rare for them, isn't it?"
"We designed the clothes to show that Mizuki, having been accepted by Niigo, can now be a source of strength"
"for others who have similar worries or who can't love themselves."
"Inspired by the story of Mizuki thinking about their future and wanting a job in fashion,"
"we chose a simple and clean one-piece dress to represent the future they're starting to envision,"
"a future that's finally coming into view."
"As a special touch, we added the yellow daisy embroidery used in Mizuki's new unit outfit"
"to convey the message of being yourself."
"It's a simple design unique to Mizuki."
"To match the song's calm, warm impression and Mizuki's faint expectations for the future,"
"we used soft and gentle colors," they said.
It's true, the colors for everyone are very soft overall.
That's right. Did everyone notice the daisies? They're there.
The daisies are there.
I see comments about the daisies now.
The blue traffic light was also very striking.
That's true.
Mizuki was born in summer, August 20th... Happy Birthday!
I didn't mean to say it so casually, but Happy Birthday.
I feel like Mizuki hasn't had many summer-like illustrations until now.
They usually wear long sleeves or clothes that cover their skin.
